topic: soil drainage, root depth, and vine vigour

steps.label: cause
steps.text: Soil texture and composition influence how effectively water drains through the vineyard soil.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: In well-drained soils, vine roots must grow deeper to find water, which limits excessive vine vigour and reduces the plant's resources available for canopy growth.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: Lower vine vigour can lead to more concentrated fruit, with better flavour concentration and generally higher grape quality potential.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Well-drained soil supports grape quality because it encourages deeper roots and limits vigour; the vine can therefore produce more concentrated fruit.
